source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two wires of materials A and B are joined end to end and fixed at both ends with tension all along the wires at 1024 N. The lengths, areas of cross section and densities of the wires A and B are 0.6 m, 0.5 m, 10^{-5} m2, 2 \times 10^{-5} m2, 4000 kg m^{-3}, 8000 kg m^{-3} respectively. Determine the lowest frequency of a transverse wave which sets up standing waves in the wires with a node at the joint C.

image_dependency: The question text already provides the key numerical values and setup in words, so image dependence appears helpful but not absolute
multi_step: Solving requires combining data for two different wire segments A and B
The setup implies multiple physics steps: derive linear mass densities from area and density, derive wave speeds from tension, and impose standing-wave/node conditions at the joint
The phrase 'lowest frequency' suggests harmonic compatibility across both wire segments rather than a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a concrete numeric answer '400 Hz'
Multiple-choice options are provided and include '400 Hz'
The result is formula-based and can be numerically checked from the provided parameters

risk_flags: modality_signal_conflict: requires_image=true but question_text appears largely self-contained

decision_reasons: 资产完整：题干、答案和图像均存在，registry_passed=true，且未记录 issues
初步评分较高：multi_step_score=0.86，verifiability_score=0.95，具有较好的题目质量与可校验性
答案为明确的短文本数值答案，可直接验证，适合进入候选池
虽存在 modality_signal_conflict 风险标记，但这反映的是图像依赖判断上的轻微信号冲突，不构成核心语义缺失或不可恢复问题
